Tully Coffee'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-25-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kari Erickson Levine, Esq. and Julie Ann Arbuckle, Esq. of SEYFARTH SHAW 397-2823 for defendant TULLY'S COFFEE CORPORATION moving party
John Houston Scott, Esq. of PRENTICE & SCOTT 292-1660 for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as set forth below with the following exception -- GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISCRIMINATION AS TO DEFENDANT JOHN ROSELLINI and also grant summary adjudication as to Rosellini on the claim for punitive damages.
Defendant TULLY's COFFEE's motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication:
1st Cause of Action (Disability Discrimination) -- granted, no triable issue of material fact that Tully's failed to reasonably accommodate Plaintiff GREENE's disability (eye condition or stress-related disability).
2nd Cause of Action (sexual orientation discrimination) -- summary adjudication granted. Plaintiff failed to argue in opposition as to this claim.
3rd Cause of Action (harassment, hostile work environment) -- denied, triable issue of material fact as to whether the alleged conduct was sufficienly severe or pervasive and whether it was directed at Plaintiff GREENE because of her gender or sexual orientation.
4th Cause of Action (retaliation) -- denied, triable issue of material fact exists whetehr Defendant employees were aware that Plaintiff GREENE had engaged in a protected activity, i.e. complained of discrimination, so there is a triable issue of material fact as to causal link between protected activity and the adverse action. Also, triable issue of material fact exists that written and oral reprimands after complaints of discrimination were used as basis to substantially alter terms and condtions of employment in retaliation for complaints of discrimination. (kh) Hon. James J. McBride. Reporter: Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855
